Hey JB here, time for your daily motivation slash skills trading. I want to talk to you about health and wellness, my own health most. Listen, I want to just tell you something, that I got sick this weekend. I never really get sick much, right? I'm like one of these people like, I think that my body has like, you know, has been hardened to this spot where like the germs don't look at me as a hospitable environment. Like, you know what I'm saying? Like a virus needs a host, right? Like, you know, in the, you know, you've watched zombie movies like World War Z, right? Well, the solution to getting rid of the zombies is you inject yourself with like some virus and like even the zombies are scared. So, oh, damn, you guys got typists. I'm not, you know, going near that guy. They don't bite you. So you become invisible to the zombies. I kind of made my body like that through all the years of abuse. So when the viruses, you know, come around like they always land on my wife and she, how hard it is, like, oh, come enjoy the party here. It's healthy inside. Nothing bad going on here, right? And then so she gets sick, right? Then we'll kiss and we'll make it right. They come into my bed and they jump out. They're scared, right? So, but I did get a little bit sick. I admit that I got, she had strep throat actually, which is a serious infection, right? So I got my own version of strep throat. So while she was out for seven days with, you know, dying, I was out for like 12 hours dying, right? And I was very, very sick, but my body, you know, beat it back. But I'll be honest with you, like I didn't wake up feeling great today. My first day back, right? I was really sick. I was, damn, sick yesterday. I was like, you know, sweating all the way. But you know, I said to myself as a man, I don't mean a man, a woman. I mean, as an adult, I realized that, you know, hey, you know, I'm not going to be the sort of person that allows himself or herself to use excuses like that from not showing up and doing the things they know I have to do. I'm just telling you, I don't care whether you're old or young. As you get older, you're going to have less and less days where you feel perfect. And I was saying to myself that exact thing this morning, saying, you know, like, you know, a lot of these days that I don't feel great, but I never let it stop me from performing at the highest level. Most of the time when you don't feel great and you're not healthy, it's like you're not optimally healthy. There's a very big difference between being how I am now where I'm like an 80% of optimal health and how I was yesterday. When you're really sick, I mean, yeah, you're sick. You know, sleep me alone. I'm going to shiver under the covers. I can't move. That's a different story. That's sick. And then there's like, I'm under the weather. I'm not 100%. But I'll be damned. I'm going to let that stop me from going out there and seizing the moment and going after my own life. You know, life passes your by. It just does. If you don't go out there vigilantly to go up to your own hopes and dreams and to seize those hopes and dreams every single day, it's like almost like, you know, one after the next, you could keep pushing it back and back and back before you know it. It's like the days go by, the weeks go by, the month gets faster and faster. And you're basically shitting a lock. You know, life has passed you by. You have to make every day count. You just do. When you're in sales as a commission based sales person or you're an entrepreneur, same thing, same thing, right? You have to make a decision. Who do you want to be? Where do you want to live? Meaning what are your standards to this stuff? Are you going to allow, you know, suboptimum feelings of perceived feelings of health and wellness, get in your way, allow that to be your narrative for the day? I'm not feeling well. So I don't, what? Don't perform at the highest level. Don't call on as many people. Don't do my callbacks. You can do that. I force myself to do the opposite. I force myself to work twice as hard on the days where I know I'm not up to par physically. I train myself over the years to do that. And you know what happens? You start feeling better. As you start moving and season the day, you start realizing that your own brain is so powerful that when you don't allow your body to be sick, and I mean not the real sick. I mean to be subpar, your body will cooperate. All of a sudden the aches seem to go away. The feeling of tarnus and lethargy disappears. It's replaced by one of motivation and feeling great. And then you start feeling like you accomplished even more. Because you start feeling really good about yourself. You're like, damn, you know, most people would have written off that day, not me. And you start to realize who you are and what you're capable of. So much of success is this inner assurance that we feel. It's not an assurance we feel that we're best and we're meant to be great. And if you try to talk that into yourself, but you're not walking the walk, you're not doing what great people do, which means pushing through. You push through your slumps. You push through your sick days. You push through your downturn. You push through the emotional worries you have about life and relationships and everything else, all the things that can clutter your mind, get in the way of your focus and you blast through that because you know that it'll always be better and always be easier to deal with that stuff with more money in your pocket. That's one thing I can promise you. I don't care if you're sick or whatever that issue is, it's going to be easier to deal with when you're wealthy. You got to make money. You got to solve that money problem and life, my friend, is way of just tick, tick, tick. It passes you by. The older you get, the faster that clock takes away. Don't make one day a bad day because you were just like, I had to sniffles. I had to stop it. Grow up. That's what we did in Ferris Bueller's Day off when you're 15, 16. Yeah, that's the stuff you tell your parents to get out of school one day. It's not how we live our lives as adults. You're kidding. No one but yourself. You know when you're too sick to work and then you do what you should do. Lie down, take medication and get better. But don't kid yourself. When you're playing that under the weather game and talking it into yourself, I can't work. You know what? It's just both. It's the story that you tell yourself and it stops you from getting what you want. It stops you from being all you can be. Why is it that some people just, you never hear of that sick. They just show up to work. It's not that their immune system is strong. By the way, maybe that's like 5% of the story. Mostly it's they refuse to let that be their narrative. This is simply a gift you give yourself to show up. I promise you this. When you start training yourself to do that and you know that it's just really not an excuse to me, guess what? You're going to start feeling better every day. You're going to start feeling more energetic because you know it's just not a way out for you. You can complain and bitch and moan but you know what? You're going to work anyway. You're showing up anyway. Why complain about it? Why even let it entertain you? You get up, you take a deep breath. You don't tell people your tale of woe. Tell them that you don't see life and watch how much respect you'll have for you and watch how much you respect yourself. That's the first step towards a level above where you are. This is to simply always show up. Promise you. Love you all. Talk to you tomorrow.
